title: eriogonum grande subsp. timorum
intro: Eriogonum grande subsp. timorum, also known as the Desert Willow or Mormon Tea, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and promote overall well-being. Eriogonum grande subsp. timorum can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by soaking its leaves in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
